zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire於1420年出生在英國藍卡爾埃克爾斯時，他的父親Sir Robert Booth38歲，母親Douce Venables24歲。他於1443年與Matilda Dutton在英國爾達頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭藍卡爾艾克斯頓被列為sheriff。他去世於1477年4月6日在英國藍卡爾埃克爾斯享年57歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire于1420年出生在英国藍卡爾埃克爾斯时，他的父亲Sir Robert Booth38岁，母亲Douce Venables24岁。他于1443年与Matilda Dutton在英国爾達頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰藍卡爾艾克斯頓被列为sheriff。他去世于1477年4月6日在英国藍卡爾埃克爾斯享年57岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾。

nameMeanings.name: Boothe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Booth
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Eccles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Eccles in Lancashire, or possibly from any of several other places so named in Kent, Norfolk, Berwickshire, and Dumfriesshire, from British eglēs ‘Romano-British Christian church’ (from Latin ecclesia, Greek ekklēsia ‘gathering, assembly’). Such places would have been the sites of notable pre-Anglo-Saxon churches or Christian places of communal worship.

variants.name: Exley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from Exley in Southowram (Yorkshire), from British eglēs ‘Romano-British Christian church’ (from Latin ecclesia, Greek ekklēsia ‘gathering, assembly’) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Eccles .
Americanized form of South German   Oechsle  .

variants.name: Echols
meanings: Altered form of English   Eccles   and possibly also of German   Eckels  .

variants.name: Eckles
meanings: English: variant of   Eccles  . This form is now rare in Britain.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire was born in 1420, in Eccles, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir Robert Booth, was 38 and his mother, Douce Venables, was 24. He married Matilda Dutton in 1443, in Dutton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. His occupation is listed as sheriff in Eccleston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 6 April 1477, in Eccles, Lancashire, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire est né en 1420, à Eccles, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Booth, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Douce Venables, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Matilda Dutton en 1443, à Dutton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff à Eccleston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1477, à Eccles, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire 1420 in Eccles, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert Booth 38 und seine Mutter Douce Venables 24. Er hat 1443 in Dutton, Cheshire, England Matilda Dutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff in Eccleston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 6. April 1477 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Eccles, Lancashire, England und wurde in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire è nato il nel 1420 nel Eccles, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert Booth, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Douce Venables, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Matilda Dutton nel 1443, nel Dutton, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff nel Eccleston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 6 aprile 1477, nel Eccles, Lancashire, England, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashireが1420年にイギリスランカシュアイアエククレスで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Boothは38歳，母Douce Venablesは24歳であった。1443年にイギリスチェシュアイアダットオンでMatilda Duttonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアエククレストンでの職業はsheriffと記録されている。彼は1477年4月6日にイギリスランカシュアイアエククレスで57歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアトラフォード マノーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire(이)가 1420년 영국 랭카실르 c클스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Booth(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Douce Venables(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1443년 영국 체실르 드턴에서 Matilda Dutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 치레스턴에서 sheriff이었다. 그는 1477년 4월 6일 영국 랭카실르 c클스에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire nasceu em 1420, em Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Booth, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Douce Venables, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda Dutton em 1443, em Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff em Eccleston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 6 de abril de 1477, em Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire родился в 1420 г. в местности экклесь, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Booth было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Douce Venables, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Dutton в 1443 г. в местности дуттон, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии sheriff в местности экклестон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 6 апреля 1477 г. в местности экклесь, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Eccles Boothe Knight of Garter, Sheriff of Lancashire nació en 1420, en Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Booth, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Douce Venables, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Matilda Dutton en 1443, en Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff en Eccleston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 6 de abril de 1477, en Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
